relations.reason: Highest-scoring relation candidate (score=0.9999), with coaxial cylinder alignment, near-zero gap (0.0002), identical radius (1.5821), and strong face-pair evidence including one coaxial-cylinder pair with score 0.9999.

subassemblies.reason: Top subassembly candidate with internal score 0.9999, directly derived from strongest relation; pin-in-hole insertion is a canonical subassembly pattern.

grasp_targets.reason: Highest grasp score (0.7017) among all candidates; small cylindrical pin with planar grip pair suitable for parallel jaw gripper; likely early assembly step due to simplicity.
Second-highest grasp score (0.7001); identical geometry to node-8, also a pin; may be assembled similarly after or alongside node-8.

sequence_outline: Insert pins (node-8, node-9) into holes in base
Attach Dovetail Clip (node-2) onto base via planar contact
Install screws (node-3 to node-7) into threaded holes

evidence_request.why: Although relation:node-9__node-10 has highest score, the face-pair evidence shows one coaxial pair (gap=0.0002) but two planar-coincident pairs with larger gaps (2.38 and 2.70). Visual confirmation is needed to verify whether this is a true insertion (pin fully seated) or just proximity. Also, to distinguish between pin vs screw insertion mechanics.
